transcript: Welcome to the Module 5 Hands-on Practice. You have completed all seven lessons. Now it is time to put everything together into one real deliverable. This exercise asks you to choose a business scenario, design a complete funnel in Miro, identify what is broken and how to fix it, and prepare a client-ready presentation walkthrough. Let's walk through the steps. Here is what you are building. You will create a complete three-stage marketing funnel in Miro for a chosen business scenario. The funnel must cover every stage, how people discover the business, how they build trust, and how they convert. You will then analyze it for bottlenecks, document your fixes, and prepare your presentation using the funnel presentation prep guide from Lesson 5.7. This single deliverable synthesizes all seven lessons in this module and becomes a professional portfolio piece you can show to future clients. Before you start, gather your tools. You need access to Miro, a free account works perfectly, and the funnel presentation prep guide from Lesson 5.7. You can build on the Miro board you created in Lesson 5 or start a fresh board for this exercise For your business scenario choose one of four options An online fitness coach a marketing consultant a local dental practice or a real client you are currently working with. Option D is always the most valuable. Use a real scenario whenever possible. Set aside 60 to 90 minutes to complete all six steps without rushing. Step number one is to choose your business scenario and write a one-sentence profile describing who they are, what they sell, and who their ideal customer is. Use your persona knowledge from lesson 2.4 to make this specific. Step number two is to select the correct funnel type from lesson 5.2 and write one sentence justifying your choice. Use this quick guide. If the business needs to build a lead list first, choose a lead generation funnel. If they are ready to sell directly, choose a sales funnel. If they use education to convert, choose a webinar funnel. Write your justification before you open Miro. The funnel type determines everything about the structure you are about to build. Step number three is to map the complete funnel in Miro. Use the sample board as your structural reference. Your board needs at least three touch points in the awareness stage at least three in the consideration stage and at least two in the decision stage Connect every touch point with directional arrows and label each touch point with the Go High Level tool that powers it For example, GHL landing page at the lead capture point or GHL email sequence in the nurture stage. Apply the step-by-step build process from Lesson 5.5 to build each zone methodically before connecting them. Step number four. Once your funnel is mapped, apply the bottleneck framework from Lesson 5.6. Identify at least two points where leaves are most likely to drop off. Add a sticky note beside each one on the mural board with the problem in one sentence and your proposed fix in one sentence. Be specific. Name the stage, the drop-off reason, and the exact optimization you would implement. Step number five is to prepare your board for client presentation. Clean up all working annotations. Replace every technical label with plain client-friendly language. And create narrow frames for each section of your walkthrough. One for the overview, one per stage, and one for the full flow summary. Step number six is to complete the funnel presentation prep guide from lesson 5.7. Fill in all six sections Then if you can record a five practice walkthrough using Miro presentation mode Present to a colleague a family member or record yourself and review the playback Hearing yourself deliver the five-part framework out loud once before a real client meeting makes an enormous difference to your confidence on the day. Before marking module 5 complete, verify your work against this checklist. Confirm your scenario profile and funnel type justification are written. Confirm your Miro board has the minimum touchpoint counts and go high level labels. Confirm at least two bottleneck sticky notes are on the board with both a problem and a fix. Confirm your board is cleaned up with frames created for presentation and confirm your funnel presentation prep guide is fully completed. Check all boxes and you are done. Module 5 is complete. You can now design a marketing funnel from scratch. Identify what is holding it back and present it to a client with the confidence of a strategic partner. Save your Miro board and your prep guide to your portfolio folder right now. Then move to Module 6, Creating Effective Email and SMS Campaigns, where you will build the nurture content that powers the M of U stage of the exact funnel you just designed. Well done!

visualDescriptions.description:
The image presents a screenshot of an educational presentation slide, likely from an online course or tutorial. The title "What You'll Build" is prominently displayed in bold black text at the top left corner.

**Project Description:**

* A white box on the left side of the image contains a green header labeled "PROJECT DESCRIPTION."
* The description outlines a project where students will design a complete, client-ready marketing funnel in Miro for a chosen business scenario.
* The funnel must cover all three stages - TOFU, MOFU, and BOFU - with labeled touchpoints, automation notes, and GoHighLevel references.
* Students will then analyze the funnel for bottlenecks, document their optimization recommendations, and prepare a 15-minute client presentation walkthrough.
* This exercise synthesizes Lessons 5.1 through 5.7 (stages, mapping, Miro design, analysis, and presentation).

**Why This Matters:**

* A gray box on the right side of the image features a star icon and the heading "WHY THIS MATTERS."
* Two bullet points explain the significance of the project:
	+ In real client work, this is the exact deliverable you present before any GoHighLevel build begins.
	+ A completed Miro funnel board is a professional portfolio piece that demonstrates strategic thinking.

**Visual Elements:**

* A green bar runs along the top of the image, with the word "Libre" in green text in the top-right corner.
* A yellow and orange graphic is visible in the bottom-right corner, accompanied by a green progress bar labeled "SYNTHESIZING MODULE 5."

Overall, the image appears to be a screenshot from an online course or tutorial, providing instructions and context for a marketing funnel project.

The image depicts a screenshot of a digital flowchart, likely created using Miro, a collaborative online whiteboard platform. The flowchart is titled "FreshBites Lead Generation Funnel Map" and appears to illustrate the process of generating leads for a product or service called FreshBites.

**Flowchart Structure:**

* The flowchart is divided into two main sections: 
  * Consideration Stage (left)
  * Decision Stage (right)

**Key Elements:**

* The flowchart features various shapes, including:
  * Circles
  * Diamonds
  * Rectangles
  * Arrows

These shapes are used to represent different stages and decisions in the lead generation process.

**Stages and Decisions:**

* The flowchart outlines the following stages and decisions:
  + Consideration Stage: This stage appears to involve evaluating options and considering the product or service.
  + Decision Stage: This stage seems to involve making a decision about whether to proceed with the product or service.

**Color-Coded Labels:**

* The flowchart uses color-coded labels to categorize different types of content:
  + Green: likely represents positive or successful outcomes
  + Yellow: may represent neutral or uncertain outcomes
  + Orange: could represent challenges or obstacles

**Software Interface:**

* The top of the image shows the Miro interface, including:
  + Menu bar
  + Toolbar
  + Zoom control (20%) 

Overall, the image provides a visual representation of the lead generation process for FreshBites, highlighting the key stages, decisions, and outcomes involved in converting leads into customers.

The image presents a checklist titled "Success Checklist" in bold black text at the top left, accompanied by the green logo "Libre" in the top-right corner. The checklist is divided into two columns and four rows, with a green checkmark preceding the title.

*   **Verify your work before marking Module 5 complete**
    *   This section serves as a reminder to verify work before marking Module 5 complete.
*   **Checklist Items**
    *   **Business scenario selected and one-sentence profile written**
        *   Step 1
    *   **Correct funnel type identified with one-sentence justification**
        *   Step 2 - Lesson 5.2
    *   **Complete three-stage Miro funnel board with minimum touchpoint counts met**
        *   Step 3 - Lessons 5.3, 5.4, 5.5
    *   **GoHighLevel tool references labeled on relevant touchpoints**
        *   Step 3 - Lesson 5.1
    *   **At least two bottlenecks identified and sticky notes added with problem and fix**
        *   Step 4 - Lesson 5.6
    *   **Miro board cleaned up and Frames created for client presentation**
        *   Step 5 - Lesson 5.7
    *   **Funnel Presentation Prep Guide completed in full**
        *   Step 6 - Lesson 5.7
    *   **Practice walkthrough delivered or recorded**
        *   Step 6 Bonus - Lesson 5.7
*   **MODULE 5 MILESTONE**
    *   If all boxes are checked, you have completed Module 5 and have a professional funnel design ready for your portfolio.

The checklist provides a clear and organized guide for completing Module 5, ensuring that all necessary steps are taken to achieve a professional funnel design.
